root_causes: ECC scrubbing storm triggered under sustained memory bandwidth load
Driver 550.54.14 does not throttle ECC scrub rate on B0 stepping
failure_chain: memory_bandwidth test saturates HBM2e bus at 98% utilization
ECC background scrub competes for memory controller bandwidth
Scrub storm causes 340ms stall on memory controller arbitration
Test runner timeout (300ms) exceeded before result returned
Test marked FAILED: timeout waiting for device response
rca.impact: 8 of 12 nodes on B0/550.54.14 reproduce this failure
rca.summary: ECC scrubbing storm on B0 stepping under sustained memory bandwidth load causes memory controller stalls that exceed test runner timeout thresholds. Reproduces consistently on driver 550.54.14 across all B0 nodes in this run.

remediation_suggestions.action: Pin driver to 545.29.06 for all B0 stepping nodes
steps: Identify all farm nodes running silicon_revision=B0
Roll back driver from 550.54.14 to 545.29.06 on affected nodes
Re-run memory_bandwidth suite to confirm resolution
File regression ticket against 550.54.14 referencing HW-ERRATA-2891

remediation_suggestions.action: Increase test runner timeout from 300ms to 800ms as temporary mitigation
steps: Update TEST_RUNNER_TIMEOUT_MS=800 in farm node environment config
Restart test runner service on affected nodes
Re-run memory_bandwidth suite to verify reduced failure rate

systemic_issues.common_root_cause: ECC scrubbing storm under sustained memory bandwidth load — known B0 stepping errata
systemic_issues.recommended_action: Pin all B0 nodes to driver 545.29.06 pending errata fix in 551.x release
